top_findings: CRITICAL: The core 'business listing' page type effectively does not exist in production. Every 'view business' link sitewide points to one hardcoded demo page (/business/newa-lahana). lib/seo-auto/sitemaps.ts::getListingSitemapEntries() returns a single hardcoded entry instead of enumerating real listings, and no app/business/[slug]/page.tsx dynamic route exists. Homepage claims '50,000+ local businesses'; sitemap-listings-1.xml has 1 URL.

CRITICAL SECURITY (non-SEO): app/login/page.tsx ships hardcoded admin credentials (superadmin/superadmin, admin/admin) in client-side JavaScript with client-only localStorage 'auth' and no server-side session verification. /super-admin/* returns HTTP 200 with no server-side auth gate. The public nav links directly to this login path.

CRITICAL: 12 live blog posts (5 near-duplicate topic clusters, 85-90% text overlap, published 1-2 days apart) are indexed via llms.txt/RSS but absent from the XML sitemap, indicating the auto-blog 'embedding dedup' pipeline is not preventing near-duplicate republishing.

HIGH: Host canonicalization relies solely on rel=canonical -- nepalidirectory.com (non-www) serves HTTP 200 directly instead of a 308 redirect to www, creating two fully crawlable copies of every page.
HIGH: 34 of 103 pages (33%) share the identical fallback title 'Nepali Directory | Nepal's trusted local business directory' because they never set page-specific metadata.
HIGH: Zero security headers beyond HSTS (no CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy) on a site with login/admin/UGC flows.
HIGH: Live sitemap.xml exposes all 10 /super-admin/* and /admin/ai URLs (noindexed but not disallowed in robots.txt) -- already fixed in current repo source but not deployed to production.
quick_wins: Redeploy origin/main to production -- current repo source already fixes the admin-sitemap leakage and robots.txt gaps; production is stale at an older commit.
Add a 308 host redirect (non-www -> www) at the Vercel/middleware edge layer.
Add unique metadata.title/description to the 21 public static pages inheriting the homepage default.
Fix the malformed priceRange: "Rs Rs Rs" value in the Restaurant schema template (also a visible UI bug on the business page).
Add a next.config.ts headers() block for CSP/X-Frame-Options/X-Content-Type-Options/Referrer-Policy.
Remove the 'Super Admin' link from the public mobile navigation and replace client-only auth with real server-verified sessions.

categories.name: Technical SEO
categories.note: Blended from a holistic technical audit (findings/technical.md, 64/100 across 9 sub-areas) and a dedicated sitemap/crawlability deep-dive (findings/sitemap.md, 28/100) that surfaced the audit's most severe issue. See both files for full detail.
what_works: robots.txt explicitly allows Googlebot, Bingbot, OAI-SearchBot, ChatGPT-User, GPTBot, PerplexityBot, ClaudeBot
All 103 crawled URLs return HTTP 200, well-formed XML sitemaps, correct 404 handling
Canonical tags present and correct on 100% of crawled pages
Clean, human-readable URL structure with no query-string pollution
Current repo source already contains fixes for the admin-sitemap leakage and robots.txt gaps -- just needs deployment

findings.title: Core business-listing page type does not exist in production
findings.description: lib/seo-auto/sitemaps.ts::getListingSitemapEntries() hardcodes a single entry; no app/business/[slug]/page.tsx dynamic route exists; only app/business/newa-lahana/page.tsx (static demo) is live.
findings.recommendation: Wire getListingSitemapEntries() to lib/enrich/factory.ts::createListingRepository() and build app/business/[slug]/page.tsx with generateMetadata() and LocalBusiness/Restaurant schema. Full code-level plan in findings/sitemap.md section 1.

findings.title: Hardcoded admin credentials in client-side JavaScript
findings.description: app/login/page.tsx contains literal 'superadmin'/'superadmin' and 'admin'/'admin' credential checks shipped in the browser bundle, with localStorage-only session state and no server-side verification. /super-admin/* returns HTTP 200 with no server-side gate.
findings.recommendation: Replace with Supabase Auth (already wired via utils/supabase/) enforced server-side in middleware.ts before any /super-admin or /admin route renders. Remove the public nav link to it.

findings.title: Host canonicalization missing a hard redirect
findings.description: nepalidirectory.com (non-www) returns HTTP 200 directly with byte-identical content to www, mitigated only by rel=canonical, not a 308 redirect. No host-redirect logic exists anywhere in the codebase.
findings.recommendation: Add a Vercel host-based redirect rule or a middleware.ts host check returning a 308 before Supabase session logic runs.

findings.title: Admin panel URLs exposed in the public sitemap and not disallowed in robots.txt
findings.description: Live sitemap.xml lists all 10 /super-admin/* + /admin/ai URLs; robots.txt does not disallow /admin/ or /super-admin/. Already fixed in current repo source (lib/seo-config.ts, app/robots.ts) but production is deployed at a stale commit.
findings.recommendation: Trigger a fresh Vercel production deployment and verify the live diff post-deploy.

findings.title: No security headers beyond HSTS
findings.description: No CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, or Permissions-Policy on a site with login/admin/UGC flows (write-review, claim-listing, ask-question).
findings.recommendation: Add a headers() block in next.config.ts or extend middleware.ts to set these site-wide.

categories.name: Content Quality
what_works: 14 original blog posts with consistent, well-formed unique metadata and og/twitter tags
Author-desk attribution system (Health Desk, Food Desk, etc.) with Organization-typed schema and knowsAbout arrays
Custom ai-content-declaration meta tag signaling human-reviewed content -- good transparency practice

findings.title: 12 blog posts form 5 near-duplicate content clusters (85-90% text overlap)
findings.description: Verified via direct text diff: 3 posts on 'comparing local services before booking' published 1-2 days apart share 686-703 of ~800 five-word phrase sequences. Same pattern across 4 more topic clusters. The project's auto-blog 'embedding dedup' is not preventing this.
findings.recommendation: Fix the dedup similarity threshold in the blog cron; consolidate each cluster to one canonical post via redirect.

findings.title: Blog posts are thin for their topic competitiveness (500-660 words)
findings.description: Commercial-investigation topics (dental clinics, contractors) run 504-658 words with generic, interchangeable advice rather than specific local detail.
findings.recommendation: Expand highest-intent posts with concrete NPR price ranges, named neighborhoods, and (once available) real listing references.

findings.title: Author bylines are organizational desks, not named individuals with credentials
findings.description: Healthcare content (YMYL-adjacent) is bylined to 'Nepali Directory Health Desk' rather than a named reviewer with stated qualifications.
findings.recommendation: Add a named human reviewer byline for healthcare content specifically.

categories.name: On-Page SEO
what_works: Sound title-template architecture in app/layout.tsx
100% of crawled pages have a meta description and og:title
0 pages exceed the meta description upper bound meaningfully

findings.title: 34 pages inherit the homepage's default title/meta instead of page-specific values
findings.description: 21 public static pages plus 10 admin pages never export their own metadata, falling back to the root layout default and creating a large duplicate-title cluster.
findings.recommendation: Add unique metadata to each of the 21 public static route files.

findings.title: 9 pages have a duplicated 'Nepali Directory' brand suffix in the title
findings.description: /attribution and 8 /authors/* pages include the brand name in their own title string, which then gets the layout template's suffix appended a second time.
findings.recommendation: Remove the manual brand-name inclusion from these 9 pages' title strings.

findings.title: 55 of 103 titles exceed 60 characters, risking SERP truncation
findings.description: Author desk page titles run 77-92 characters, cutting off the brand suffix in typical search result display.
findings.recommendation: Shorten author desk titles to keep unique content within ~50-55 characters.

categories.name: Schema / Structured Data
what_works: Organization + WebSite schema on all 103 crawled pages
Contextual types layered correctly: FAQPage (34), BreadcrumbList (33), ItemList (30), CollectionPage (25), BlogPosting/Article, ProfilePage
The one live Restaurant listing has a genuinely thorough schema graph: address, aggregateRating, hasMenu with real NPR-priced MenuItems, amenityFeature, openingHoursSpecification

findings.title: priceRange value is malformed ("Rs Rs Rs")
findings.description: Not a valid schema.org priceRange format; also renders visibly broken in the UI next to the star rating.
findings.recommendation: Use standard $-style tier symbols or a real numeric range derived from the existing MenuItem offer prices.

findings.title: openingHoursSpecification only covers 4 of 7 days with no explanation
findings.description: Tuesday, Friday, Saturday are silently absent rather than marked closed.
findings.recommendation: Populate all 7 days or add explicit closed markers before this template is reused at scale.

findings.title: No GeoCoordinates on the listing schema
findings.description: Full PostalAddress exists but no geo lat/long, limiting map-adjacent rich-result eligibility.
findings.recommendation: Add geo to the listing schema template before scaling to more listings.

categories.name: Performance (Core Web Vitals)
categories.note: Lab-only estimate from source/response inspection -- no Google PageSpeed/CrUX API credentials were configured for this audit.
what_works: next/image used throughout with responsive srcset, lazy loading, and automatic WebP negotiation
images.remotePatterns tightly scoped to images.unsplash.com only
Brotli compression and Vercel edge caching active; webfont preloaded and self-hosted via next/font

findings.title: TTFB inconsistent, up to 1.7s on some routes
findings.description: Median ~980ms across 103 URLs from this crawl; expectation for cached/prerendered routes is sub-200ms.
findings.recommendation: Verify with real CrUX/PageSpeed field data; confirm ISR/static caching is consistently hit in production.

findings.title: Large HTML payloads on hub pages (up to 193KB)
findings.description: City/category grid pages ship verbose repeated srcset markup per card image.
findings.recommendation: Trim srcset breakpoint counts for small grid thumbnails; consider pagination once real listing volume grows.

categories.name: AI Search Readiness (GEO)
what_works: robots.txt explicitly allows every major AI crawler
A genuinely well-built llms.txt with organized sections and per-link descriptions written for LLM consumers
FAQ schema and clear H2 structure across blog/comparison content aids passage-level extraction

findings.title: llms.txt actively links to 12 near-duplicate blog posts across 5 topic clusters
findings.description: AI crawlers following llms.txt's own guidance will hit 2-3 near-identical answers per topic with no canonical signal, undermining the citability the file is trying to build.
findings.recommendation: Fix the dedup pipeline, consolidate clusters, then curate llms.txt's Local Guides section to one entry per topic.

findings.title: Individual-business AI citability capped at 1 real page
findings.description: Same root cause as the audit-wide Critical finding -- AI engines have zero citable pages for the vast majority of real local business queries.
findings.recommendation: Prioritize the listings fix; re-run a GEO citability check once real listings exist.

categories.name: Images
what_works: All 301 crawled tags have non-empty alt text
next/image with full responsive srcset, lazy loading, and automatic WebP delivery confirmed via Accept-header negotiation
OG images present with explicit width/height/alt on homepage and blog posts

findings.title: All production imagery is generic Unsplash stock photography, not real business photos
findings.description: Every hero/card image sampled across city, category, and blog pages is stock photography, undermining trust/E-E-A-T for a 'verified local directory' positioning.
findings.recommendation: Prioritize owner-uploaded photos in the listing-claim flow once real listings launch.

findings.title: Homepage OG image is just the 512x512 logo, not a branded social card
findings.description: Undersized for link-preview rendering on social/messaging platforms versus the ideal 1200x630.
findings.recommendation: Generate a dedicated branded 1200x630 OG image for the homepage.

supplementary_categories.name: Sitemap Architecture (deep-dive)
supplementary_categories.note: Not a top-level weighted category per the scoring model -- folded into Technical SEO's blended score, but reported separately because it contains the audit's single most severe, most thoroughly root-caused finding. See findings/sitemap.md for the full code-level fix plan.

supplementary_categories.name: Local SEO
supplementary_categories.note: Platform-level local landing page and NAP-template assessment. See findings/local.md.

supplementary_categories.name: Content Cluster / Architecture
supplementary_categories.note: Hub-and-spoke interlinking maturity between blog and category/city pages. See findings/cluster.md.

supplementary_categories.name: Backlink Profile
supplementary_categories.note: Informational floor, not a diagnostic failure -- this is an expected, unconcerning score for a pre-launch site with no link-building activity yet. No Moz/Bing API credentials configured; Common Crawl tier only. See findings/backlinks.md.

phases.name: Phase 1: Critical Fixes
items: Redeploy origin/main to production (fixes admin-sitemap leakage and robots.txt gaps that are already coded but not live)
Fix hardcoded admin credentials in app/login/page.tsx; implement real server-side auth via Supabase before /super-admin and /admin routes render
Add a 308 host redirect from nepalidirectory.com to www.nepalidirectory.com
Wire lib/seo-auto/sitemaps.ts::getListingSitemapEntries() to the real listing repository and build app/business/[slug]/page.tsx
Fix the auto-blog dedup pipeline and consolidate the 5 near-duplicate topic clusters (12 posts) to one canonical post each

phases.name: Phase 2: High-Impact Improvements
items: Add unique metadata.title/description to the 21 public static pages sharing the homepage default title
Add a next.config.ts headers() block for CSP/X-Frame-Options/X-Content-Type-Options/Referrer-Policy
Fix the priceRange: "Rs Rs Rs" schema/UI bug
Remove the public 'Super Admin' nav link
Fix the 9 pages with duplicated brand-suffix titles; shorten the 55 titles exceeding 60 characters

phases.name: Phase 3: Content & Authority
items: Expand the highest commercial-intent blog posts with concrete local specifics (prices, neighborhoods)
Add named human reviewer bylines for healthcare content
Differentiate the 8 city pages beyond the current ~85%-shared template
Add GeoCoordinates and complete openingHoursSpecification to the listing schema template
Begin real link-building outreach (Nepal tourism boards, business associations, claimed-business backlinks)

phases.name: Phase 4: Monitoring & Iteration
items: Set up Google PageSpeed Insights / CrUX / Search Console API access (free) to replace lab-only performance and indexation estimates with real field data
Re-run this audit's GEO and SXO categories once real listings exist to measure the post-fix improvement
Add ItemList schema to city pages once real listing data exists to populate it
Establish a sitemap pagination scheme (sitemap-listings-2.xml, etc.) before listing volume approaches a few thousand URLs
